work_unit: H-001 upstream proxy OOM body cap (audit-2026-07-06-026 derived_fix)
situation: audit-2026-07-06-026 H-001 finding: dev-server.js /api/health reverse-proxy accumulates upstream response body with no size cap (line 105-107 `b += c` unbounded). Malicious or faulty upstream returning GB-scale body would OOM the dev-server process. /healthz contract is KB-scale JSON; legitimate responses never exceed a few KB.
action: Added MAX_UPSTREAM_BODY = 64KB constant at module scope. In data handler, check b.length + c.length > cap before accumulation; if exceeded, set oversized flag, destroy upstream stream (triggers error → fail() path returning 502 with diagnostic 'upstream response too large'). Existing responded-guard and fail() helper reused — no new response paths introduced. 41/41 tests pass in worktree after fix.
outcome_evidence: worktree commit 7001a07 on auto/opt-dev-server-1783353888 pushed to origin; npm test in worktree: 41 pass 0 fail (test #37 /api/health proxy still passes, confirming normal <64KB responses unaffected); main working tree restored clean by opt-worktree.sh post-commit.
next_suggestion: 派生 M-001 (ONEDAY_DIR arbitrary dir read) — next highest severity pending fix from audit-2026-07-06-026
派生 M-002 (/api/* static collision) after M-001
L-001 MIME gaps lowest priority — defer until H/M cleared

audit_findings.finding: Upstream proxy response body accumulated without size cap — OOM DoS vector
audit_findings.remediation: Cap at 64KB; destroy stream + 502 on exceed
